# Quality Gates — Web / Frontend

_Applies when: finishing any React / frontend change — the web overlay on `30-quality.md`._

Web-only additions to the neutral DoD in `30-quality.md` — this never restates it. The generic
green gate, the `verify` step, and the Definition of Done live there; only the web-specific gates
are below.

## Full green gate — web specifics

- `lint` **with FSD boundary checks** (`--max-warnings=0`) — the web-specific gate layered on the
  generic lint / typecheck / full-suite in `30-quality.md`. Wire it as a Husky pre-commit hook +
  lint-staged (`eslint --fix` + Prettier) where possible.
- **Boundary lint fails open**: `eslint-plugin-boundaries` silently drops imports it cannot
  resolve — green at severity `error`. Per fail-loud (`30-quality.md`), trust the file count it
  reports, never its exit code. And keep the boundaries canary OUT of any test-glob location where
  the rule is off — `**/__tests__/**` overrides commonly disable boundaries — so the fixture lives
  in the slice root and only the test sits under `__tests__/`.
- `build` succeeds (Turbo). For offline single-file tools, the build must stay
  `file://`-compatible (no external CDN; inline SVG/CSS).
- Code-duplication budget: keep < 5% (jscpd/cpd in CI; minTokens 50, minLines 5).
- No hardcoded values — config lives in `shared/config/`.

## UI work — verify visually (mandatory)

- "Green tests" is not done. Open the UI in a browser (claude-in-chrome / Playwright)
  and **look at it**; capture screenshots.
- Never ship generic, mediocre, AI-looking UI. Aim for graphically appealing,
  high-end, design-led output — use the design / impeccable / frontend-design skills.
  A build that passes but looks flat or broken is a defect, not a pass.
- Gotchas: `file://` is blocked by the browser tool (serve via `http.server`); rAF
  freezes in hidden tabs; ensure demo data loads so rich states are visible; rebuild
  before re-testing to avoid a stale single-file.
- Accessibility is a gate: WCAG AA, keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels,
  respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
